action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 3 diagnostics confirmed checkpoints/best.pt is a ViT model saved with torch.compile() (_orig_mod prefix). predict.py has already been updated to strip this prefix. Training curves from iter 2 show 10 epochs completed successfully. Analysis: The only failure in iter 2 was checkpoint loading during evaluation. With the fixed predict.py, the trained ViT checkpoint should evaluate correctly. We need the ViT baseline score to decide whether to scale this approach or pivot. Decision: Submit evaluation immediately using the existing checkpoint and fixed predict.py. This costs ~0 training time and gives us the CLIP ViT baseline.
action.command: python -c "import torch, timm; m=timm.create_model('vit_base_patch16_clip_224.openai', pretrained=False, num_classes=10); sd=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); nsd={k[10:] if k.startswith('_orig_mod.') else k: v for k,v in sd.items()}; m.load_state_dict(nsd, strict=False); print('Checkpoint loads OK')"
